description: Deterministic Routing Policy and Safety Constraints for Edge Smart Home with Class 2 clarification/transition semantics
semantic_change_summary: Class 2 is no longer interpreted only as terminal caregiver escalation.

Class 2 is a clarification/transition state for insufficient context, unresolved ambiguity, stale policy-relevant state, missing critical state, actuation failure, or caregiver-required sensitive paths.
The LLM may generate bounded candidate choices and user-facing guidance, but it must not make final class decisions, authorize actuation, or trigger emergency handling by itself.
Class 2 may transition to Class 1, Class 0, or Safe Deferral / Caregiver Confirmation only after user/caregiver confirmation or deterministic evidence.

class_0_emergency.description: Life-critical emergencies requiring immediate deterministic override. Class 0 may be reached directly from emergency evidence or after Class 2 clarification if the user/caregiver confirms emergency help or deterministic emergency evidence arrives.

triggers.interpretation_note: This emergency is triggered by trigger_event semantics rather than by a dedicated environmental_context field.

class_1_low_risk.description: Bounded low-risk assistance handled by local LLM candidate generation and Deterministic Validator. Class 1 may be reached directly or after Class 2 clarification if the user/caregiver confirms a low-risk assistance candidate and deterministic validation resolves exactly one admissible action.

interpretation_notes.single_admissible_action_rule: Autonomous execution is permitted only when deterministic validation resolves exactly one admissible low-risk action.
interpretation_notes.ambiguity_rule: If multiple admissible candidates remain or target confidence is insufficient, the system must prefer safe_deferral or Class 2 clarification rather than autonomous actuation.
interpretation_notes.staleness_rule: Freshness violations in policy-relevant state must not be silently tolerated in the low-risk path.
interpretation_notes.class2_transition_rule: A Class 2-to-Class 1 transition is allowed only after user/caregiver confirmation and deterministic validation against the low-risk catalog.

class_2_clarification_transition.description: Clarification and transition state for unresolved ambiguity, insufficient context, stale policy-relevant state, missing critical context, actuation failure, or caregiver-required sensitive paths. This state may produce bounded candidate choices and route to Class 1, Class 0, or Safe Deferral / Caregiver Confirmation after confirmation or deterministic evidence.

allowed_transition_targets.condition: User or caregiver confirms a bounded low-risk assistance candidate and Deterministic Validator approves exactly one admissible action from the low-risk catalog.
User/caregiver confirms emergency help, an emergency-pattern input such as triple-hit occurs, or deterministic E001-E005 emergency evidence arrives.
No response, ambiguous response, persistent insufficient context, unresolved conflict, stale critical state, missing critical state, or actuation failure remains unresolved.

implementation_notes.terminology_note: Canonical terminology should use Class 2 clarification/transition state and class2_clarification_manager for insufficient-context interaction, while preserving context-integrity-based safe deferral terminology for the lower-level deferral handler.
implementation_notes.note: The routing policy may retain a summarized allowed_actions_taxonomy for local readability, but the dedicated low-risk action catalog remains the authoritative action list.
implementation_notes.safe_execution_note: Validated execution must remain ACK-confirmed and bounded to the low-risk catalog.
implementation_notes.class2_semantic_note: Class 2 is a clarification/transition state. It may end in Class 1, Class 0, or Safe Deferral / Caregiver Confirmation depending on confirmation or deterministic evidence.
implementation_notes.llm_boundary_note: The LLM may generate bounded candidate choices and guidance, but it cannot make final class decisions, authorize actuation, trigger emergency handling, approve sensitive actuation, or bypass Policy Router / Deterministic Validator.
implementation_notes.sensitive_actuation_note: Sensitive actuation requests, including doorlock control, are not part of the Class 1 autonomous low-risk action scope in this policy version. Such requests must not be approved as low-risk executable payloads and must be handled through Class 2 clarification/escalation or a separately governed manual confirmation path.
implementation_notes.doorbell_context_note: Doorbell or visitor-arrival detection may be used as policy-relevant context for visitor-response interpretation, but it does not authorize autonomous doorlock control. Doorlock-related sensitive actuation must remain routed to Class 2 clarification/escalation or a separately governed manual confirmation path.
implementation_notes.mqtt_payload_governance_note: MQTT topic entries, topic-payload mappings, interface-matrix alignment reports, topic-drift reports, and payload validation reports are governance/verification artifacts and must not be interpreted as policy, validator, caregiver approval, audit, actuator, or doorlock execution authority.
implementation_notes.reviewer_note: This policy table defines routing and safety interpretation, while detailed action admissibility remains jointly constrained by the low-risk action catalog and validator schema.
